title: Citadel of Qaitbay
description: A 15th-century defensive fortress located on the Mediterranean coast.

title: Catacombs of Kom El Shoqafa
description: A massive necropolis consisting of a series of Alexandrian tombs merging Pharaonic and Greco-Roman styles.

title: Pompey's Pillar (Serapeum)
description: A Roman triumphal column dedicated to Diocletian, standing on the site of the ancient Serapeum.

title: Roman Amphitheatre (Kom El-Dikka)
description: The only known Roman amphitheatre in Egypt, featuring marble seats and mosaic flooring.

title: Villa of the Birds
description: An exceptionally preserved Roman villa within the Kom El-Dikka complex, famous for its intricate mosaics.

title: Catacombs of Mustafa Kamel
description: Four subterranean tombs dating back to the 3rd and 2nd centuries BC.

title: Catacombs of Shatby
description: Considered the oldest uncovered tomb complex in Alexandria, modeled to resemble ancient Greek houses.

title: Anfushi Tombs
description: Limestone tombs painted to simulate marble and alabaster, located on the Pharos island.

title: El-Nabih Cistern
description: A hidden gem; one of the few remaining ancient cisterns, featuring columns and arches.

title: Kom El-Nadura
description: A shallow archaeological hill used as a watchtower during the Napoleonic campaign.

title: Taposiris Magna (Temple of Osiris)
description: A large temple complex and necropolis aimed at Osiris, rumored burial place of Cleopatra.

title: Montaza Palace (Al-Haramlik)
description: A mixture of Ottoman and Florentine architecture, the summer palace of the former royal family.

title: Montaza Tea Kiosk
description: A charming pavilion on the island within Montaza gardens, used by the King for afternoon tea.

title: Ras El Tin Palace
description: The oldest royal palace in modern Egypt, overlooking the western harbor.

title: Royal Jewelry Museum
description: Housed in an exquisite palace, displaying the jewelry and artifacts of the Muhammad Ali Dynasty.

title: Alexandria National Museum
description: A restored Italianate villa containing artifacts from the Pharaonic, Greco-Roman, Coptic, and Islamic eras.

title: Antoniadis Palace
description: Modeled after the Palace of Versailles, located within historic gardens with archaeological remains.

title: Tomb of Antoniadis Garden
description: A hidden rock-cut tomb located deep within the Antoniadis Gardens.

title: Terbana Mosque
description: A hanging mosque known for using ancient columns from earlier Greek/Roman sites.

title: El-Shorbagi Mosque
description: Located in the old Turkish district, featuring distinctive Ottoman architecture and tiles.

title: Abu al-Abbas al-Mursi Mosque
description: The largest mosque in Alexandria, dedicated to the Andalusian Sufi saint.

title: Imam Al-Busiri Mosque
description: Dedicated to the poet Al-Busiri, famous for his poem Al-Burda.

title: Eliyahu Hanavi Synagogue
description: One of the largest synagogues in the Middle East, symbolizing Alexandria's cosmopolitan past.

title: St. Mark's Coptic Orthodox Cathedral
description: The historical seat of the Pope of Alexandria, founded by St. Mark the Evangelist.

title: Church of the Sacred Heart
description: A striking Neo-Gothic Catholic church famous for its stained glass and high spire.

title: Greek Orthodox Cathedral
description: A magnificent Neo-Gothic church serving the Greek community.

title: Cavafy Museum
description: The preserved apartment of the famous Greek-Alexandrian poet Constantine Cavafy.

title: Shallalat Gardens (City Walls)
description: Contains a surviving segment of the old city walls and the medieval Tower of the Romans.

title: Greco-Roman Museum (Building)
description: The historic building housing relics from the 3rd century BC to the 7th century AD.

title: Cecil Hotel
description: A historic hotel that hosted Winston Churchill and Al Capone, central to the Alexandria Quartet.

title: Windsor Palace Hotel
description: A historic luxury hotel overlooking the Eastern Harbor.

title: Villa Ambron
description: The villa where writer Lawrence Durrell lived during WWII.

title: Necropolis of Gabbari
description: A massive necropolis known as the City of the Dead, largely hidden under modern infrastructure.

title: Unknown Soldier Memorial
description: Originally a monument to Khedive Ismail, now dedicated to the Unknown Naval Soldier.

title: Chatby War Memorial Cemetery
description: The final resting place for soldiers of the British Empire who died in the World Wars.

title: Al-Qaaed Ibrahim Mosque
description: Designed by Italian architect Mario Rossi, showcasing a fusion of Mamluk and Italian styles.

title: Okalle El-Lemoon
description: One of the last surviving Okallas (commercial caravanserais) in Alexandria.

title: Cistern of Ibn Battuta
description: An ancient water storage system located near the stadium.

title: Alexandria Train Station
description: The primary railway terminus, an architectural landmark featuring impressive steel arches.

title: Abusir Tower
description: A replica of the Pharos lighthouse used as a funerary monument in Taposiris Magna.

title: Fort of Tabiyet Nahassin
description: Parts of the copper fortifications used during the Muhammad Ali era.

title: Museum of Fine Arts
description: Housing Egyptian modern art and hosting the Alexandria Biennale.

title: Baron Menasce Villa
description: The historic residence of a prominent family, architecturally significant.

title: Cordahi Complex
description: A residential block exemplifying the cosmopolitan neo-classical architecture of the era.

title: Bibliotheca Alexandrina
description: The modern library built near the site of the ancient Royal Library.

title: Sidi Bishr Mosque
description: A significant local mosque named after Sheikh Bishr.

title: Sunken Cities of Aboukir
description: The submerged ruins of Thonis-Heracleion and Canopus in Abu Qir Bay.

title: Stanley Bridge
description: An iconic architectural landmark mimicking royal palace styles.

title: Saint Catherine Cathedral
description: The apostolic vicariate of Alexandria, burial place of King Victor Emmanuel III.

title: Monastery of Saint Mina
description: A UNESCO-listed archaeological site containing church ruins and baths.
